Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>繼續</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-10</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

However, I am the memory agent observing the primary session. The message indicates that the user in the primary session said "繼續" (continue).

Without actual tool executions, file operations, or other concrete actions from the primary session, I cannot create meaningful observations. The user just said "continue" but I don't see what they're continuing.

The instruction says: "No output necessary if skipping."

So I should not output anything at this time since there's no concrete work to observe.

content.thinking:
The user is continuing from a previous session. I can see concrete work being done now:

1. Read operations on `scripts/check-streaming-token-strip.mjs` - checking streaming token tests
2. Grep operations on `src/sessionManager.ts` - looking at `toolPrefixLines` function and `TOOL_STATUS_EMOJI` constants
3. Write operation creating a new test file `scripts/check-draft-render.mjs`

The new file is a comprehensive regression test for draft rendering in Telegram. The comments are in Chinese and explain a specific issue that was fixed on 2026-07-10:

The issue was about draft streaming display in Telegram. The Telegram client has an animation based on "length difference" semantics - it immediately displays the first `strlen(prev)` characters of the new text, then fades in the last `max(0, strlen(new) - strlen(prev))` characters. 

The old `renderReply()` put tools/thinking at the top, so when a new tool line completed and characters were inserted at the top, the client would immediately show a prefix that was missing the same length of characters from the end of the body (users saw displayed text "rolling back").

The fix was `renderDraftReply()` which reorders to: body → thinking → tools, making draft frames approximately append-only, and outputs markdown (not HTML).

The test file validates:
1. Order: body → thinking → tools
2. Stable prefix: when tools increase, body prefix doesn't change (prevents animation rollback)
3. Body appending = prefix extension
4. Markdown output (not HTML, no escaping)
5. Empty case returns empty string
6. Long body truncation keeps tail
7. Token stripping works same as renderReply

This is a bugfix observation - fixing the display rollback issue in Telegram draft streaming.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed Telegram draft streaming display rollback</title>
  <subtitle>Reordered draft rendering to body → thinking → tools for stable prefix during streaming updates</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>scripts/check-draft-render.mjs created as regression test for 2026-07-10 draft streaming display issue</fact>
    <fact>Telegram client uses length-difference animation semantics: immediately shows first strlen(prev) chars, fades in remaining</fact>
    <fact>Old renderReply() placed tools/thinking at top, causing body text rollback when new tool lines inserted at head</fact>
    <fact>New renderDraftReply() orders content as body → thinking → tools for append-only draft frames</fact>
    <fact>renderDraftReply() outputs markdown text instead of HTML for Telegram's rich_message.markdown field</fact>
    <fact>Test validates 7 behaviors: section ordering, prefix stability on tool growth, body append prefix extension, markdown preservation, empty case, long body truncation, and token stripping</fact>
    <fact>toolPrefixLines() in src/sessionManager.ts generates tool status lines with emoji (⚙️ running, ✅ completed, ❌ failed)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed a visual regression in Telegram draft streaming where users saw displayed text roll backwards during agent tool execution. The root cause was Telegram's client-side animation behavior: when updating draft text, it immediately renders the first N characters (where N = previous text length) and fades in any additional characters. The old renderReply() function placed tool status lines and thinking indicators at the top of the message. When a new tool completed, this inserted characters at the message head, causing the immediately-visible prefix to clip the same number of characters from the body tail—users saw their readable text disappear and reappear.

The fix introduced renderDraftReply() with reordered sections: body text first, then thinking indicator, then tool timeline. This makes draft updates approximately append-only, so the common prefix between consecutive frames covers the entire previous body. The function also outputs raw markdown instead of HTML (matching Telegram's rich_message.markdown field requirements), preserves tail content when truncating long messages, and strips special tokens (SKILL_USED, SCHEDULE) consistently with the final render path.

A comprehensive regression test suite was created covering ordering invariants, prefix stability under tool timeline growth, markdown vs HTML output, empty cases, truncation behavior, and token stripping correctness.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>scripts/check-streaming-token-strip.mjs</file>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>scripts/check-draft-render.mjs</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

The check-draft-render.mjs suite verifies the core fix: body → thinking → tools ordering prevents the visual rollback bug where Telegram's length-difference animation would clip body text when tool status lines were inserted at the message head. Tests confirm prefix stability (adding tools doesn't change the body prefix), append-only behavior for body updates, markdown output (not HTML-escaped), proper truncation of long messages, and consistent token stripping.

The check-draft-streaming.mjs suite validates the API layer: trySendDraft() correctly calls sendMessageDraft for plain text or sendRichMessageDraft for rich content, sends required parameters (chat_id, draft_id, markdown), handles API errors gracefully, maintains draft_id consistency across multiple frames, and properly finalizes drafts into messages. All 40 assertions passed, confirming the fix works end-to-end from render logic through Telegram API integration.</narrative>

↔ ACP Agent (Kiro/Codex/Claude)。\\nBridge 負責 session 管理、preamble 注入、token 解析、enrichment pipeline、MCP memory。\\n主入口 `src/index.ts`（~1467 行），指令 handler 拆至 `src/commands/`，核心邏輯拆至獨立模組。\\n\\n## 模組分工\\n\\n- COMMAND_SPECS + keyboard builders + registerBotCommands\\n- `run-prompt.ts` — runPrompt 核心（streaming edit、token dispatch、goal continuation、specialist proxy）\\n- `working-state.ts` — WorkingState 寫入 + 共用依賴注入（避免循環 import）\\n- `memory.ts` — preamble 建構（buildPreambleWithBreakdown）+ fact CRUD\\n- `sessionManager.ts` — ACP session lifecycle（create/close/idle sweep）\\n- `acpClient.ts` — ACP protocol adapter（spawn agent、send prompt、stream response）\\n- `observerTransformer.ts` — 解析 agent 回覆中的 token（ASK/SEND_FILE/SCHEDULE/STICKER/...）\\n- `specialist.ts` + `specialist-create.ts` — 本機 specialist 分身（proxy/delegate/parallel）\\n- `enrichment.ts` — 每 turn 動態注入（NotebookLM routing + wiki retrieval + memory recall）\\n- `embed-router.ts` — BGE-small-zh embedding + cosine similarity（共用向量引擎）\\n- `facts-store.ts` — master log + topic shard 讀寫\\n- `mcp-memory.ts` — MCP Memory server（remember/list_facts/forget/propose_topics/...）\\n- `mcp-google.ts` + `google-api.ts` — Google Drive/Docs 與 Maps MCP；Workspace\\n  使用可攜式 refresh token，Maps 使用受 API 限制的獨立 key\\n- `goalStore.ts` — /goal 狀態機\\n- `scheduler.ts` + `recurrence.ts` — 排程系統\\n- `daily-intel/` + `commands/intel.ts` — 可設定 Profile 的每日情報模組；collectors、SQLite、dedup、ephemeral ACP structured analysis、ranking、Markdown/JSON report、Telegram delivery 與 Scheduler 排程\\n- `relay.ts` — 跨機 agent 通訊（Telegram bot-to-bot + Redis）\\n- `local-llm.ts` — Ollama 本地模型整合（分類/摘要/壓縮）\\n- `page-index.ts` — PageIndex 樹索引式長文檢索（ingest_prepare/ingest_save）\\n- `backup.ts` — /backup robocopy 鏡像\\n\\n## 慣例\\n\\n- TypeScript strict mode，ESM（`\\\"type\\\": \\\"module\\\"` in package.json）\\n- 改完 .ts 後跑 `node scripts/tsc-json.mjs` 驗證 type check\\n- Daily Intel 多檔改動仍需跑正式 `npm.cmd run build`；`tsc-json.mjs` 曾漏報缺少 type import，不能單獨作為完成依據\\n- `scripts/check-daily-intel.mjs` 直接 import `dist`，因此必須在 `npm.cmd run build` 完成後再執行；兩者不可平行，否則 smoke 可能讀到舊 build 產生假失敗\\n- Config 全在 `src/config.ts`，讀 `.env`\\n- Bridge runtime config（`dream.json`、`specialist-domains.json`、`specialists.json`、`relay-peers.json`、`notebooklm-routing.json`）讀取優先 `${MEMORY_DIR}/config/`，fallback `${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/`；新建/寫入一律走 `${MEMORY_DIR}/config/`。`AGENT_CONFIG_DIR` 仍只代表 agent CLI 自己的設定目錄。\\n- 新功能先開 `docs/SPEC-<slug>.md`，完工後歸檔\\n- Daily Intel 設定在 `config/intel-profiles.json` 與 `config/intel-sources.json`；Profile 不保存時間，排程只由 ScheduleStore 管理\\n- Daily Intel 內建 `ai` 與 `game-industry` 兩個 profile，各有至少 5 個固定官方來源；新增領域應沿用同一 pipeline，不建立領域專用 service\\n- Daily Intel 核心來源成功 batch 以 SQLite `source_batch_cache` 快取 6 小時，跨 profile 重用收集結果但仍各自分析；失敗 batch 不可寫 cache\\n- Daily Intel retry 依賴 SQLite `run_items`：analyze/report 重用原 run 資料，deliver 只重送 `report_artifacts`；不可用「完整新 run」取代所有 retry，否則 dedup 會跳過待重試 item\\n- Daily Intel `/intel retry-source` 只重跑原 run `metrics.errors` 中指定的失敗核心來源，並建立獨立新 run；不可連帶重抓已成功來源\\n- Daily Intel `/intel search` 查詢既有 item 全文與 analysis 欄位，需 escape SQL LIKE wildcard 並限制查詢長度/回傳筆數\\n- Daily Intel event cluster 人工 merge/split 只修改 SQLite cluster/member；既有 report artifact 保持不可變，避免 Telegram 已送內容與磁碟檔案被靜默改寫\\n- Daily Intel weekly trend 依 `pipeline_runs.started_at` 計算 UTC 7 日窗口，每個 item/profile 只取最新 analysis，避免 prompt version 重複計數\\n- Daily Intel 兩個內建 profile 的 `report.outputLanguage` 固定為 `zh-TW`；摘要、claim、專案影響、建議行動、聚類差異與報告固定標籤使用繁體中文，來源標題/產品名/URL 可保留原文\\n- Daily Intel 不只靠 prompt 要求中文；analysis 與 cluster parser 會拒絕完全不含漢字的生成文字並觸發既有 retry，避免英文內容寫入 DB\\n- Daily Intel 自訂主題由 `/intel topic add <name> --keywords <csv>` 建立，使用 `topic-` profile、嚴格 24 小時發布日期、最多 10 篇、繁中報告，並自動建立目前聊天室每日 09:00 排程\\n- Daily Intel `topic-ai` 是手動維護的 AI digest profile 例外：固定來源包含 Google News、AI newsletter、arXiv、官方/產業 RSS，`lookbackHours=72`、`maxHighlights=30`、`report.selectionMode=video`；topic-only RSS 不加通用 `ai` tag，避免污染內建 `ai` profile 的 5 個官方核心來源\\n- `report.selectionMode=video` 會用 story-ready heuristics 重新排序報告候選：強調具體數字/金額、agent/資安/成本衝突、行動建議、討論性與類別多樣性；預設 `score` 模式維持舊行為，一般 `/intel topic add` 仍使用 `score`\\n- Daily Intel 影片產生模式由 profile `report.videoMode` 決定：`podcast` 保留既有 NotebookLM Audio Overview→封面 MP4→YouTube 流程，`notebooklm` 改用 NotebookLM Video Overview 產影片簡報；`report.selectionMode` 只管選題排序，不管產生模式。\\n- `query.publishedAtPolicy=strict` 會在 raw/item 入庫前同時過濾核心與探索來源；缺發布日期、超出 lookback 或未來日期都不進分析與報告，內建 profile 明確使用 `none`\\n- 自訂主題可用 `/intel topic source <profile-id> <add|remove> <source-id>` 綁定既有 Source Catalog 固定來源；探索仍受原有預算、信任折扣與報告占比限制\\n- Daily Intel Exploration Lane 只在 ephemeral ACP session 注入 Agent Browser MCP；候選保存於 `discovered_sources`，探索失敗不得使核心來源 run 失敗，報告需套用 trust discount 與占比上限\\n- Daily Intel 探索頁面需允許最多 2MB HTML；500KB 會讓 AP/Business Insider 等正文在解析前被頁面殼誤殺。啟用 Agent Browser MCP 時，探索 HTTP 失敗會改走 Browser collector。\\n- Daily Intel Browser source 必須使用內建版本化 `scriptId` + `domainAllowlist`；預設 DB、報告、browser 診斷與子 agent scratch memory 寫入 repo `output/daily-intel/`，不要放 `${MEMORY_DIR}`（可用 `INTEL_*` env override）。\\n- Daily Intel event clustering 是 analyze stage 的 best-effort 子步驟；cluster 持久化供 report retry 重用，primary source 由程式依 trust/score/time 選擇\\n- Daily Intel relevance 使用 profile JSON 的 bounded `relevanceContext`（goals/projects/watchFor/ignore），不重用完整聊天 enrichment 或 frozen preamble\\n- Daily Intel `/intel run` 完成後會自動 draft learning candidates，但只透過 `IL:*` inline buttons 人工批准後才寫入 memory/wiki/shared；candidate 本身是 pipeline artifact，保存在 Daily Intel SQLite。\\n- Shared knowledge repo 用 `/sharedsync` 發佈本機 `${MEMORY_DIR}/shared` 變更：先 commit、本機 rebase pull、push，push 被拒時 rebase 後重試一次；`/sharedpull` 保持只拉取遠端。兩者若 pull 到新/更新的 `.md`，會在同一 session 觸發 meta prompt 請 agent 讀檔並判斷是否值得內化為 memory/wiki。\\n- Daily Intel metrics 的 token 數為字元估算並標記 `estimated`；ACP adapters 未提供一致 billing usage 時 `estimatedCostUsd` 保持 null，不偽造費用\\n- Daily Intel Podcast 是 report 後的獨立非同步 job，不可阻塞或改變 pipeline run 狀態；音訊/MP4 只能放 repo `output/tmp` 類暫存目錄，禁止放 `${MEMORY_DIR}`，YouTube 成功後立即刪除。\\n- Podcast 封面不可直接假設為偶數尺寸；FFmpeg 轉 H.264 前固定 scale/pad 到 1280x720，否則 `libx264` 會因奇數高度拒絕輸出。\\n- NotebookLM `studio_poll` 的 `media_url` 可能因 connector 固定欄位解析而為 null；Podcast 需用 `gArtLc` raw RPC 從 artifact 巢狀 `audio/mp4` variants 解析。媒體下載前以 `npm run auth:notebooklm-media` 保存完整 Google cookie jar，不能只依賴 connector 舊版 cookie string。\\n- NotebookLM MCP `video_overview_create` 在 `notebooklm-mcp-server@3.0.7` 會用舊 payload 假成功 `artifact:null`；用 `node scripts/patch-notebooklm-video-overview.mjs` 修補全域 dist 後，新啟動的 MCP process 可建立 Video Overview artifact，`studio_poll` 也能從巢狀 video variants 回 `media_url`。既有已連線 MCP process 需重啟才會載入 patch。\\n- NotebookLM MCP 已 vendored 成獨立本地 server：`F:\\\\AI_Codex\\\\notebooklm-mcp-server`；Codex config 的 `[mcp_servers.notebooklm]` 直接跑 `node F:\\\\AI_Codex\\\\notebooklm-mcp-server\\\\dist\\\\index.js server`，避免重裝全域 npm package 覆蓋 Video Overview 修補。\\n- Daily Intel Telegram 管理指令修改 Profile/Source 時，必須重新驗證完整 config 並透過 `atomicJson.updateJson` 原子寫回，成功後才更新記憶體狀態\\n- Policy 文件放 `POLICIES/`，三邊 agent 共用\\n\\n## 踩坑\\n\\n- `src/index.ts` 仍有 ~1467 行（callback_query dispatcher + specialist proxy glue + enrichment pipeline），改動前先 grep 定位\\n- Split 進度：P0-P6 全部完成（6471→1467 行，-77%），SPEC 已歸檔 done\\n- preamble 是 frozen snapshot（session 內不重建），見 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md`\\n- Windows 環境：讀檔用 read tool 不用 PowerShell，見 `POLICIES/windows-shell-safety.md`\\n- 繁中 Markdown 在 PowerShell `Get-Content` 可能顯示成亂碼；以 `rtk read`/dedicated read tool 判斷實際內容，docs 改完可跑 `node scripts/check-doc-encoding.mjs`。\\n- strReplace 有 silent-fail 風險，改完要 read 驗證\\n- Relay `RELAY_DONE` 只關閉 loop guard 並移除 token，不可壓掉最後一段非空 auto reply；否則 bot-to-bot 對話結束時群組看不到 closing reply。\\n- Relay 委派會跨 owner private chat 與 relay group session；incoming/outgoing delegate、RELAY_INPUT、RELAY_OUTPUT、RELAY_RESULT 要寫 `relay-task-ledger.json`，且 `RELAY_INPUT` 必須注入 relay group session。\\n- Relay transport 支援 `redis`、`telegram`、`both`；`telegram` mode 走 Telegram bot-to-bot 並刻意停用 Redis poller，`both` 表示 Telegram primary + Redis fallback，不是雙送。\\n- `/relay status` 顯示 relay transport、Telegram/Redis 設定狀態、poller 預期狀態與 loop guard；不得輸出 Redis URL/token 或 bot token。\\n- `TG_RICH_STREAM_ENABLED` 預設開啟；streaming placeholder 先嘗試 Rich draft seam，沒有 Rich API 或 API 失敗就回到既有 `editMessageText`。\\n- Reaction Control 監聽 `message_reaction`；`👍/👎/✅/👀` 第一版只寫 `reaction_feedback` 低風險事件，`✅` 不直接結束 `/goal`。\\n- Telegram bot-to-bot live probe 用 `node scripts/check-relay-telegram-live.mjs` dry-run；加 `--send` 才真正呼叫 Telegram Bot API，會送 unique `[RELAY:<bot>]` probe 並嘗試 sender-side group mirror。\\n\\n## 工具\\n- **codegraph**：已建索引，追呼叫鏈用 `codegraph_context` / `codegraph_trace`\\n- **領域索引**：`.codegraph/domains.json` 定義 12 個領域，codegraph 查詢結果會自動附加 domain 資訊和相關 wiki 連結\\n\\n## 測試\\n\\n```bash\\nnode scripts/tsc-json.mjs          # type check\\nnode scripts/run-all-smoke.cmd     # 全部 smoke test\\nnode scripts/check-<name>.mjs     # 單支 smoke test\\nnode scripts/check-daily-intel.mjs # Daily Intel profile/command/schedule smoke\\nnode scripts/check-google-api.mjs  # Google API request + MCP tool smoke\\nnode scripts/check-doc-encoding.mjs\\nnode scripts/check-rich-renderer.mjs\\nnode scripts/check-reaction-control.mjs\\nnode scripts/check-relay-status.mjs\\nnode scripts/check-relay-telegram-live-smoke.mjs\\n```\\n\",\"numLines\":117,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":117}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

`/intel retry-source` 只重跑原 run `metrics.errors` 中指定的失敗核心來源，並建立獨立新 run；不可連帶重抓已成功來源\\n- Daily Intel `/intel search` 查詢既有 item 全文與 analysis 欄位，需 escape SQL LIKE wildcard 並限制查詢長度/回傳筆數\\n- Daily Intel event cluster 人工 merge/split 只修改 SQLite cluster/member；既有 report artifact 保持不可變，避免 Telegram 已送內容與磁碟檔案被靜默改寫\\n- Daily Intel weekly trend 依 `pipeline_runs.started_at` 計算 UTC 7 日窗口，每個 item/profile 只取最新 analysis，避免 prompt version 重複計數\\n- Daily Intel 兩個內建 profile 的 `report.outputLanguage` 固定為 `zh-TW`；摘要、claim、專案影響、建議行動、聚類差異與報告固定標籤使用繁體中文，來源標題/產品名/URL 可保留原文\\n- Daily Intel 不只靠 prompt 要求中文；analysis 與 cluster parser 會拒絕完全不含漢字的生成文字並觸發既有 retry，避免英文內容寫入 DB\\n- Daily Intel 自訂主題由 `/intel topic add <name> --keywords <csv>` 建立，使用 `topic-` profile、嚴格 24 小時發布日期、最多 10 篇、繁中報告，並自動建立目前聊天室每日 09:00 排程\\n- Daily Intel `topic-ai` 是手動維護的 AI digest profile 例外：固定來源包含 Google News、AI newsletter、arXiv、官方/產業 RSS，`lookbackHours=72`、`maxHighlights=30`、`report.selectionMode=video`；topic-only RSS 不加通用 `ai` tag，避免污染內建 `ai` profile 的 5 個官方核心來源\\n- `report.selectionMode=video` 會用 story-ready heuristics 重新排序報告候選：強調具體數字/金額、agent/資安/成本衝突、行動建議、討論性與類別多樣性；預設 `score` 模式維持舊行為，一般 `/intel topic add` 仍使用 `score`\\n- Daily Intel 影片產生模式由 profile `report.videoMode` 決定：`podcast` 保留既有 NotebookLM Audio Overview→封面 MP4→YouTube 流程，`notebooklm` 改用 NotebookLM Video Overview 產影片簡報；`report.selectionMode` 只管選題排序，不管產生模式。\\n- `query.publishedAtPolicy=strict` 會在 raw/item 入庫前同時過濾核心與探索來源；缺發布日期、超出 lookback 或未來日期都不進分析與報告，內建 profile 明確使用 `none`\\n- 自訂主題可用 `/intel topic source <profile-id> <add|remove> <source-id>` 綁定既有 Source Catalog 固定來源；探索仍受原有預算、信任折扣與報告占比限制\\n- Daily Intel Exploration Lane 只在 ephemeral ACP session 注入 Agent Browser MCP；候選保存於 `discovered_sources`，探索失敗不得使核心來源 run 失敗，報告需套用 trust discount 與占比上限\\n- Daily Intel 探索頁面需允許最多 2MB HTML；500KB 會讓 AP/Business Insider 等正文在解析前被頁面殼誤殺。啟用 Agent Browser MCP 時，探索 HTTP 失敗會改走 Browser collector。\\n- Daily Intel Browser source 必須使用內建版本化 `scriptId` + `domainAllowlist`；預設 DB、報告、browser 診斷與子 agent scratch memory 寫入 repo `output/daily-intel/`，不要放 `${MEMORY_DIR}`（可用 `INTEL_*` env override）。\\n- Daily Intel event clustering 是 analyze stage 的 best-effort 子步驟；cluster 持久化供 report retry 重用，primary source 由程式依 trust/score/time 選擇\\n- Daily Intel relevance 使用 profile JSON 的 bounded `relevanceContext`（goals/projects/watchFor/ignore），不重用完整聊天 enrichment 或 frozen preamble\\n- Daily Intel `/intel run` 完成後會自動 draft learning candidates，但只透過 `IL:*` inline buttons 人工批准後才寫入 memory/wiki/shared；candidate 本身是 pipeline artifact，保存在 Daily Intel SQLite。\\n- Shared knowledge repo 用 `/sharedsync` 發佈本機 `${MEMORY_DIR}/shared` 變更：先 commit、本機 rebase pull、push，push 被拒時 rebase 後重試一次；`/sharedpull` 保持只拉取遠端。兩者若 pull 到新/更新的 `.md`，會在同一 session 觸發 meta prompt 請 agent 讀檔並判斷是否值得內化為 memory/wiki。\\n- Daily Intel metrics 的 token 數為字元估算並標記 `estimated`；ACP adapters 未提供一致 billing usage 時 `estimatedCostUsd` 保持 null，不偽造費用\\n- Daily Intel Podcast 是 report 後的獨立非同步 job，不可阻塞或改變 pipeline run 狀態；音訊/MP4 只能放 repo `output/tmp` 類暫存目錄，禁止放 `${MEMORY_DIR}`，YouTube 成功後立即刪除。\\n- Podcast 封面不可直接假設為偶數尺寸；FFmpeg 轉 H.264 前固定 scale/pad 到 1280x720，否則 `libx264` 會因奇數高度拒絕輸出。\\n- NotebookLM `studio_poll` 的 `media_url` 可能因 connector 固定欄位解析而為 null；Podcast 需用 `gArtLc` raw RPC 從 artifact 巢狀 `audio/mp4` variants 解析。媒體下載前以 `npm run auth:notebooklm-media` 保存完整 Google cookie jar，不能只依賴 connector 舊版 cookie string。\\n- NotebookLM MCP `video_overview_create` 在 `notebooklm-mcp-server@3.0.7` 會用舊 payload 假成功 `artifact:null`；用 `node scripts/patch-notebooklm-video-overview.mjs` 修補全域 dist 後，新啟動的 MCP process 可建立 Video Overview artifact，`studio_poll` 也能從巢狀 video variants 回 `media_url`。既有已連線 MCP process 需重啟才會載入 patch。\\n- NotebookLM MCP 已 vendored 成獨立本地 server：`F:\\\\AI_Codex\\\\notebooklm-mcp-server`；Codex config 的 `[mcp_servers.notebooklm]` 直接跑 `node F:\\\\AI_Codex\\\\notebooklm-mcp-server\\\\dist\\\\index.js server`，避免重裝全域 npm package 覆蓋 Video Overview 修補。\\n- Daily Intel Telegram 管理指令修改 Profile/Source 時，必須重新驗證完整 config 並透過 `atomicJson.updateJson` 原子寫回，成功後才更新記憶體狀態\\n- Policy 文件放 `POLICIES/`，三邊 agent 共用\\n\\n## 踩坑\\n\\n- `src/index.ts` 仍有 ~1467 行（callback_query dispatcher + specialist proxy glue + enrichment pipeline），改動前先 grep 定位\\n- Split 進度：P0-P6 全部完成（6471→1467 行，-77%），SPEC 已歸檔 done\\n- preamble 是 frozen snapshot（session 內不重建），見 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md`\\n- Windows 環境：讀檔用 read tool 不用 PowerShell，見 `POLICIES/windows-shell-safety.md`\\n- 繁中 Markdown 在 PowerShell `Get-Content` 可能顯示成亂碼；以 `rtk read`/dedicated read tool 判斷實際內容，docs 改完可跑 `node scripts/check-doc-encoding.mjs`。\\n- strReplace 有 silent-fail 風險，改完要 read 驗證\\n- Relay `RELAY_DONE` 只關閉 loop guard 並移除 token，不可壓掉最後一段非空 auto reply；否則 bot-to-bot 對話結束時群組看不到 closing reply。\\n- Relay 委派會跨 owner private chat 與 relay group session；incoming/outgoing delegate、RELAY_INPUT、RELAY_OUTPUT、RELAY_RESULT 要寫 `relay-task-ledger.json`，且 `RELAY_INPUT` 必須注入 relay group session。\\n- Relay transport 支援 `redis`、`telegram`、`both`；`telegram` mode 走 Telegram bot-to-bot 並刻意停用 Redis poller，`both` 表示 Telegram primary + Redis fallback，不是雙送。\\n- `/relay status` 顯示 relay transport、Telegram/Redis 設定狀態、poller 預期狀態與 loop guard；不得輸出 Redis URL/token 或 bot token。\\n- `TG_RICH_STREAM_ENABLED` 預設開啟；streaming placeholder 先嘗試 Rich draft seam，沒有 Rich API 或 API 失敗就回到既有 `editMessageText`。\\n- Reaction Control 監聽 `message_reaction`；`👍/👎/✅/👀` 第一版只寫 `reaction_feedback` 低風險事件，`✅` 不直接結束 `/goal`。\\n- Telegram bot-to-bot live probe 用 `node scripts/check-relay-telegram-live.mjs` dry-run；加 `--send` 才真正呼叫 Telegram Bot API，會送 unique `[RELAY:<bot>]` probe 並嘗試 sender-side group mirror。\\n\\n## 工具\\n- **codegraph**：已建索引，追呼叫鏈用 `codegraph_context` / `codegraph_trace`\\n- **領域索引**：`.codegraph/domains.json` 定義 12 個領域，codegraph 查詢結果會自動附加 domain 資訊和相關 wiki 連結\\n\\n## 測試\\n\\n```bash\\nnode scripts/tsc-json.mjs          # type check\\nnode scripts/run-all-smoke.cmd     # 全部 smoke test\\nnode scripts/check-<name>.mjs     # 單支 smoke test\\nnode scripts/check-daily-intel.mjs # Daily Intel profile/command/schedule smoke\\nnode scripts/check-google-api.mjs  # Google API request + MCP tool Intel 探索頁面需允許最多 2MB HTML；500KB 會讓 AP/Business Insider 等正文在解析前被頁面殼誤殺。啟用 Agent Browser MCP 時，探索 HTTP 失敗會改走 Browser collector。\\n- Daily Intel Browser source 必須使用內建版本化 `scriptId` + `domainAllowlist`；預設 DB、報告、browser 診斷與子 agent scratch memory 寫入 repo `output/daily-intel/`，不要放 `${MEMORY_DIR}`（可用 `INTEL_*` env override）。\\n- Daily Intel event clustering 是 analyze stage 的 best-effort 子步驟；cluster 持久化供 report retry 重用，primary source 由程式依 trust/score/time 選擇\\n- Daily Intel relevance 使用 profile JSON 的 bounded `relevanceContext`（goals/projects/watchFor/ignore），不重用完整聊天 enrichment 或 frozen preamble\\n- memory/wiki。\\n- Daily Intel metrics 的 token 數為字元估算並標記 `estimated`；ACP adapters 未提供一致 billing usage 時 `estimatedCostUsd` 保持 null，不偽造費用\\n- Daily Intel Podcast 是 report 後的獨立非同步 job，不可阻塞或改變 pipeline run string。\\n- NotebookLM MCP `video_overview_create` 在 `notebooklm-mcp-server@3.0.7` 會用舊 payload 假成功 `artifact:null`；用 `node scripts/patch-notebooklm-video-overview.mjs` 修補全域 dist 後，新啟動的 MCP process 可建立 支援 `redis`、`telegram`、`both`；`telegram` mode 走 Telegram bot-to-bot 並刻意停用 Redis poller，`both` 表示 Telegram primary + Redis fallback，不是雙送。\\n- `/relay status` 顯示 relay transport、Telegram/Redis /memory /summary /memorytoskill + specialist-review\\n- `commands/misc.ts` — /context /usage /backup /save /wikisync /factlint 等雜項\\n- `commands/relay.ts` — relay bot handlers、relay poller init、specialist proxy、delegation context helpers\\n- `commands/deps.ts` — commands 共用依賴注入（避免循環 import）\\n- `memory.ts` — preamble 建構（buildPreambleWithBreakdown）+ fact CRUD\\n- `sessionManager.ts` — ACP session lifecycle（create/close/idle sweep）\\n- `acpClient.ts` — ACP protocol adapter（spawn agent、send prompt、stream response）\\n- `observerTransformer.ts` — 解析 agent 回覆中的 token（ASK/SEND_FILE/SCHEDULE/STICKER/...）\\n- `specialist.ts` + `specialist-create.ts` — 本機 specialist 分身（proxy/delegate/parallel）\\n- `enrichment.ts` — 每 turn 動態注入（NotebookLM routing + wiki retrieval + memory recall）\\n- `embed-router.ts` — BGE-small-zh embedding + cosine similarity（共用向量引擎）\\n- `facts-store.ts` — master log + topic shard 讀寫\\n- `mcp-memory.ts` — MCP Memory server（remember/list_facts/forget/propose_topics/...）\\n- `mcp-google.ts` + `google-api.ts` — Google Drive/Docs 與 Maps MCP；Workspace\\n  使用可攜式 refresh token，Maps 使用受 API 限制的獨立 key\\n- `goalStore.ts` — /goal 狀態機\\n- `scheduler.ts` + `recurrence.ts` — 排程系統\\n- `daily-intel/` + `commands/intel.ts` — 可設定 Profile 的每日情報模組；collectors、SQLite、dedup、ephemeral ACP structured analysis、ranking、Markdown/JSON report、Telegram delivery 與 Scheduler 排程\\n- `relay.ts` — 跨機 agent 通訊（Telegram bot-to-bot + Redis）\\n- `local-llm.ts` — Ollama 本地模型整合（分類/摘要/壓縮）\\n- `page-index.ts` — PageIndex 樹索引式長文檢索（ingest_prepare/ingest_save）\\n- `backup.ts` — /backup robocopy 鏡像\\n\\n## 慣例\\n\\n- TypeScript strict mode，ESM（`\\\"type\\\": \\\"module\\\"` in package.json）\\n- 改完 .ts 後跑 `node scripts/tsc-json.mjs` 驗證 type check\\n- Daily Intel 多檔改動仍需跑正式 `npm.cmd run build`；`tsc-json.mjs` 曾漏報缺少 type import，不能單獨作為完成依據\\n- `scripts/check-daily-intel.mjs` 直接 import `dist`，因此必須在 `npm.cmd run build` 完成後再執行；兩者不可平行，否則 smoke 可能讀到舊 build 產生假失敗\\n- Config 全在 `src/config.ts`，讀 `.env`\\n- Bridge runtime config（`dream.json`、`specialist-domains.json`、`specialists.json`、`relay-peers.json`、`notebooklm-routing.json`）讀取優先 `${MEMORY_DIR}/config/`，fallback `${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/`；新建/寫入一律走 `${MEMORY_DIR}/config/`。`AGENT_CONFIG_DIR` 仍只代表 agent CLI 自己的設定目錄。\\n- 新功能先開 `docs/SPEC-<slug>.md`，完工後歸檔\\n- Daily Intel 設定在 `config/intel-profiles.json` 與 `config/intel-sources.json`；Profile 不保存時間，排程只由 ScheduleStore 管理\\n- Daily Intel 內建 `ai` 與 `game-industry` 兩個 profile，各有至少 5 個固定官方來源；新增領域應沿用同一 pipeline，不建立領域專用 service\\n- Daily Intel 核心來源成功 batch 以 SQLite `source_batch_cache` 快取 6 小時，跨 analysis，避免 prompt version 重複計數\\n- Daily Intel 兩個內建 profile 的 `report.outputLanguage` 固定為 `zh-TW`；摘要、claim、專案影響、建議行動、聚類差異與報告固定標籤使用繁體中文，來源標題/產品名/URL 可保留原文\\n- Daily Intel 不只靠 prompt 要求中文；analysis 與 Profile/Source 時，必須重新驗證完整 config 並透過 `atomicJson.updateJson` 原子寫回，成功後才更新記憶體狀態\\n- Policy 文件放 `POLICIES/`，三邊 agent 共用\\n\\n## 踩坑\\n\\n- `src/index.ts` 仍有 ~1467 行（callback_query dispatcher + specialist content.thinking:
The primary session just documented the Telegram draft streaming fix in the main AI.md documentation. This is documenting the work that was done, specifically:

1. Explained Telegram's length-difference animation semantics
2. Documented that draft frames must use renderDraftReply() with body→thinking→tools ordering and markdown output
3. Documented that drafts have ~30s TTL and a 10s keepalive mechanism

This is a documentation change that captures the gotcha/pattern for future reference.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Documented Telegram draft animation semantics and rendering requirements</title>
  <subtitle>Added AI.md gotcha entries for draft length-difference animation and keepalive mechanism</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>AI.md踩坑 section now documents Telegram draft animation uses length-difference semantics (shows first strlen(prev) chars immediately, fades in tail)</fact>
    <fact>Documentation specifies draft frames must use renderDraftReply() with body→thinking→tools ordering and markdown output</fact>
    <fact>Using renderReply() HTML output or placing volatile sections at message head causes visible text rollback</fact>
    <fact>Drafts have ~30 second TTL and are cleared when any regular message arrives in the chat</fact>
    <fact>run-prompt.ts implements 10-second keepalive that resends with same draftId to extend lifetime</fact>
    <fact>Keepalive stops during rate limiting periods and resumes via recovery callback</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Documented the Telegram draft streaming fix and its underlying constraints in the main codebase documentation. The new entries in AI.md's gotcha section explain Telegram's client-side animation behavior (length-difference semantics where new content shows first N characters immediately and fades in the rest), the requirement to use renderDraftReply() with specific ordering to prevent visual rollback, and the draft lifecycle mechanics including 30-second TTL, automatic clearing on incoming messages, and the 10-second keepalive mechanism that extends draft lifetime during streaming responses.

This documentation captures critical knowledge for future maintenance: why drafts must be rendered with body-first ordering (not the tools-first ordering used in final messages), why markdown must be used instead of HTML, and how the keepalive interacts with rate limiting. Future developers working on streaming UX will understand these constraints without having to rediscover them through bug reports.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>AI.md</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\SPEC-draft-streaming.md\",\"old_string\":\"### Rate Limit 處理\\n\\n- 429 期間：`editNow()` 已有 early return（不變）\\n- Draft 在 30s 後過期 — 但 recovery callback 會呼叫 `editNow()` 推最新 buffer\\n- `editNow()` 的 `trySendDraft` 用同 `draftId` 呼叫 = 自動重建 draft\\n- 無需特殊 draft 過期偵測\",\"new_string\":\"### Rate Limit 處理\\n\\n- 429 期間：`editNow()` 已有 early return（不變）\\n- Draft 在 30s 後過期 — 但 recovery callback 會呼叫 `editNow()` 推最新 buffer\\n- `editNow()` 的 `trySendDraft` 用同 `draftId` 呼叫 = 自動重建 draft\\n\\n### 2026-07-10 實測修訂（原設計兩個假設被否證）\\n\\n1. **「無需特殊 draft 過期偵測」不成立**：長 tool 執行無輸出、內容不變\\n   early-return、rate limit 早退時 draft 都會過 30s TTL 消失（且該 chat 收到\\n   任何一般訊息也會立即清掉），使用者看到訊息消失又出現。\\n   → 加 10s keepalive：距上次成功送出 ≥10s 就同 `draftId` 強制重送續命\\n   （最壞間隔 20s < 30s TTL）；rate limit 期間停送，`turnFinalized` 後停止。\\n2. **draft 幀不可直接用 `renderReply()` 輸出**：client 對 draft 更新的動畫是\\n   「長度差」語意（立即顯示新文字前 strlen(prev) 字元、淡入尾端\\n   `max(0, strlen(new) - strlen(prev))` 字元），隱含 append-only 假設。\\n   `renderReply()` 把 tools/thinking 放頭部，新 tool 行插入會讓 body 尾端\\n   視覺倒退；且其輸出是 HTML，`rich_message.markdown` 收的是 markdown。\\n   → 新增 `renderDraftReply()`（`telegram-ui.ts`）：body → thinking → tools\\n   排序 + markdown 原文，draft 幀近似 append-only。\\n   Smoke：`scripts/check-draft-render.mjs`。\",\"replace_all\":false}"</parameters>

strlen(prev))` 字元），隱含 append-only 假設。\\n   `renderReply()` 把 tools/thinking 放頭部，新 tool 行插入會讓 body 尾端\\n   視覺倒退；且其輸出是 HTML，`rich_message.markdown` 收的是 markdown。\\n   → 新增 `renderDraftReply()`（`telegram-ui.ts`）：body → thinking → tools\\n   排序 + markdown 原文，draft 幀近似 append-only。\\n   Smoke：`scripts/check-draft-render.mjs`。\",\"originalFile\":\"# SPEC: Rich Message Draft Streaming\\n\\n## 目標\\n\\n將 streaming 路徑從 placeholder+editMessageText 改為 Telegram Draft API lifecycle，\\n降低 429 結構性壓力、獲得 draft 動畫 UX、消除半截 token 洩漏。\\n\\n## API 行為（已驗證）\\n\\n| 方法 | 行為 |\\n|------|------|\\n| `sendMessageDraft(chat_id, draft_id, text)` | 建立/更新 ephemeral draft（30s TTL）；同 draft_id = 全量替換+動畫；空 text = \\\"Thinking…\\\" |\\n| `sendRichMessageDraft(chat_id, draft_id, rich_message)` | 同上，rich 版 |\\n| `sendRichMessage(chat_id, rich_message, extra)` | 持久化為真正訊息（draft 自動消失）|\\n| `sendMessage(chat_id, text, extra)` | plain fallback 持久化 |\\n\\nDraft 限制：僅 private chat；無 reply_parameters（final 才有）。\\n\\n## 設計\\n\\n### Streaming 三階段\\n\\n```\\n[1] 開始 → sendMessageDraft(chatId, draftId, \\\"\\\")        // 顯示 \\\"Thinking…\\\"\\n[2] 每幀 → sendRichMessageDraft(chatId, draftId, content) // 全量替換，動畫過渡\\n[3] 結束 → sendRichMessage(chatId, final, { reply_markup }) // 持久化 + ASK 按鈕\\n```\\n\\n### Fallback chain\\n\\n```\\nDraft 可用 + private chat → Draft path [1][2][3]\\nDraft 不可用 / group / relay → 現有 placeholder+edit path（零改動）\\nDraft [2] 失敗 → 退回 editMessageText（該幀）\\nDraft [3] 失敗 → 退回 editMessageText（final edit placeholder）\\n```\\n\\n### 檔案變更清單\\n\\n#### 1. `src/telegram-rich-renderer.ts`（+2 函式）\\n\\n```typescript\\n// 新增：發送/更新 draft\\nexport async function trySendDraft(\\n  api: unknown,\\n  chatId: number,\\n  draftId: number,\\n  text: string,\\n  useRich: boolean,\\n): Promise<boolean>\\n\\n// trySendRichMessage / tryEditRichMessageDraft — 不動\\n```\\n\\n#### 2. `src/run-prompt.ts`（3 處改動）\\n\\n**2a. Placeholder 建立（~398-418 行）**\\n\\n```\\n// 新增 draft path：\\nif (tgDraftEnabled && isPrivateChat && !isRelay && !isSilentMeta) {\\n  draftId = Date.now();  // 唯一 ID\\n  await trySendDraft(api, chatId, draftId, \\\"\\\", false);  // \\\"Thinking…\\\"\\n  useDraftMode = true;\\n} else {\\n  // 現有 ctx.reply(placeholderText) 不動\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**2b. editNow()（~430-460 行）**\\n\\n```\\n// Draft path：\\nif (useDraftMode && draftId) {\\n  const ok = await trySendDraft(api, chatId, draftId, content, config.tgRichStreamEnabled);\\n  if (ok) return;\\n  // fallback: 如果有 placeholder 就 editMessageText\\n}\\n// 現有 rich edit + plain edit 不動\\n```\\n\\n**2c. Final delivery（~1010-1081 行）**\\n\\n```\\n// Draft path（single chunk）：\\nif (useDraftMode) {\\n  const richMsg = await trySendRichMessage(api, chatId, chunk + suffix, { reply_markup, reply_parameters });\\n  if (richMsg) { finalMessageId = richMsg.message_id; }\\n  else { /* fallback: sendMessage */ }\\n}\\n// 現有 placeholder edit 路徑保留為 else\\n```\\n\\n#### 3. `src/config.ts`（+1 設定）\\n\\n```typescript\\ntgDraftEnabled: env TG_DRAFT_ENABLED ?? \\\"1\\\"  // 預設開\\n```\\n\\n#### 4. `scripts/check-draft-streaming.mjs`（新增 smoke test）\\n\\n- mock API 驗證 draft lifecycle 呼叫順序\\n- fallback 路徑驗證\\n- rate limit recovery 後 draft 重建驗證\\n\\n### Rate Limit 處理\\n\\n- 429 期間：`editNow()` 已有 early return（不變）\\n- Draft 在 30s 後過期 — 但 recovery callback 會呼叫 `editNow()` 推最新 buffer\\n- `editNow()` 的 `trySendDraft` 用同 `draftId` 呼叫 = 自動重建 draft\\n- 無需特殊 draft 過期偵測\\n\\n### Multi-chunk 處理\\n\\nDraft 模式下多 chunk 的 final delivery：\\n- Chunk 0: `sendRichMessage`（持久化，draft 消失）\\n- Chunk 1..n-1: `richSendOrQueue`（不變）\\n- Chunk n: `richSendOrQueue` + reply_markup（不變）\\n\\n### 不動的東西\\n\\n- `observerTransformer.ts` — token stripping 完全不變\\n- `renderReply()` — 仍然 rebuild 整個 buffer\\n- `telegram-ui.ts` — 不動\\n- Rate limit detection (`bot-setup.ts`) — 不動\\n- 429 recovery listener — 不動（`editNow()` 內部已換路徑）\\n- Multi-chunk split logic — 不動\\n- Relay / group 路徑 — 不動\\n\\n## 行為契約\\n\\n- [ ] BC-1: Draft streaming 顯示最新 rendered content\\n- [ ] BC-2: Final `sendRichMessage` 持久化訊息（draft 消失）\\n- [ ] BC-3: ASK 按鈕出現在 final message 的 reply_markup\\n- [ ] BC-4: Token stripping 行為與現有完全一致\\n- [ ] content.thinking:
The user is updating the SPEC document to record what actually happened during implementation vs. what was originally designed. This is documenting two key discoveries from real-world testing:

1. The original assumption "no special draft expiration detection needed" was wrong - drafts expire after 30s and need keepalive
2. The original assumption that draft frames could use the same renderReply() output was wrong - needed a new renderDraftReply() function

This is a good observation to record - it's documenting discoveries made during implementation that contradicted the original design assumptions.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Draft streaming design assumptions disproven by real-world testing</title>
  <subtitle>Added post-implementation findings: draft TTL requires keepalive, and client animation requires render reordering</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>SPEC-draft-streaming.md updated with 2026-07-10 實測修訂 section documenting two falsified assumptions</fact>
    <fact>Original assumption "no special draft expiration detection needed" failed: drafts expire after 30s during long tool execution or rate limiting</fact>
    <fact>Implemented 10-second keepalive mechanism resends same draftId to extend lifetime (worst case 20s interval < 30s TTL)</fact>
    <fact>Original assumption that draft frames could use renderReply() output failed: Telegram client uses length-difference animation semantics</fact>
    <fact>Telegram animation shows first strlen(prev) chars immediately and fades in tail, causing body text rollback when tools section grows at message head</fact>
    <fact>Implemented renderDraftReply() with body → thinking → tools ordering and markdown output instead of HTML</fact>
    <fact>Regression test scripts/check-draft-render.mjs validates new rendering behavior</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Updated the draft streaming specification to document two critical discoveries made during real-world testing that contradicted the original design. The first discovery was that drafts' 30-second TTL is a real problem: during long tool execution with no output changes, content-unchanged early returns, or rate limiting periods, drafts would expire and disappear from the user's view, then reappear when updates resumed. The solution was a 10-second keepalive that forcibly resends with the same draft ID to extend the lifetime, with a worst-case 20-second interval that stays under the 30-second expiration window.

The second discovery involved Telegram's client-side draft animation behavior. The client uses "length-difference semantics" where it immediately shows the first N characters (where N equals the previous text length) and fades in any additional characters at the end. This design assumes append-only updates. The original renderReply() function placed tool status lines at the message head, so when new tools completed and inserted lines, the immediately-visible prefix would clip the same number of characters from the body tail, making users see their readable text roll backwards. The fix required creating a separate renderDraftReply() function that reorders sections (body → thinking → tools) and outputs raw markdown instead of HTML.

These findings represent genuine learning from implementation: the original design made reasonable assumptions that turned out to be wrong when tested against real API behavior and client rendering. Documenting them in the spec ensures future maintainers understand why the code is more complex than the original design suggested.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>SPEC-draft-streaming.md</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
This is a minor refinement to the draft streaming implementation. The edit adds a check to use plain mode instead of rich mode when the draft content is empty. This ensures that empty drafts behave the same way as the initial draft creation (where empty text shows server's native "Thinking..." placeholder).

This is a small implementation detail rather than a major change or discovery - it's refining the existing draft streaming feature to handle the empty content edge case correctly.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Draft streaming handles empty content with plain mode fallback</title>
  <subtitle>Empty draft content now uses plain API call to show server's native Thinking placeholder</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>src/run-prompt.ts editNow() now checks if draftContent is empty before choosing rich vs plain mode</fact>
    <fact>Empty content sends plain draft (text=undefined) which triggers server's native "Thinking…" placeholder</fact>
    <fact>Non-empty content continues using config.tgRichStreamEnabled setting for rich markdown drafts</fact>
    <fact>Change maintains consistency with initial draft creation behavior in placeholder path</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Refined the draft streaming implementation to handle empty content gracefully. When renderDraftReply() returns an empty string (e.g., when the session has no buffer, no thinking, and no tools yet), the code now explicitly uses plain mode instead of rich mode. This ensures that empty drafts behave consistently with the initial draft creation, where sending text=undefined causes Telegram's server to display its native "Thinking…" placeholder.

Without this check, an empty rich draft would have been sent, which might not trigger the same server-side placeholder behavior. The change is a defensive edge case handler that ensures the UX remains consistent throughout the streaming lifecycle—the user sees "Thinking…" both when the draft is first created and if it ever reverts to an empty state during streaming.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.thinking:
This is a bug fix for the draft keepalive mechanism. The issue is that when using an empty string `""` as the sentinel to bypass the identical check in the keepalive timer, it fails during the "Thinking..." phase because:

1. The keepalive sets `lastRendered = ""` to force a resend
2. But if the session is still in "Thinking..." phase with empty buffer/thinking/tools, `renderDraftReply()` also returns `""`
3. Then the identical check `if (draftContent === lastRendered) return;` would match and skip the resend
4. The keepalive fails to extend the draft's lifetime

The fix uses `"\x00"` (null character) as the sentinel instead, which will never match any real rendered content, ensuring the keepalive always triggers a resend.

This is a subtle edge case fix that prevents draft expiration during long "Thinking..." phases with no content yet.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed draft keepalive sentinel collision with empty content</title>
  <subtitle>Changed sentinel from empty string to null character to prevent identical check bypass failure</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Draft keepalive previously used empty string "" as sentinel to bypass identical check for forced resend</fact>
    <fact>Empty string sentinel failed during "Thinking..." phase when renderDraftReply() also returns empty string</fact>
    <fact>Identical check (draftContent === lastRendered) would match and skip keepalive resend, causing draft expiration</fact>
    <fact>Fixed by using "\x00" (null character) as sentinel, which never matches real rendered content</fact>
    <fact>Ensures keepalive successfully extends draft lifetime even when session has no visible content yet</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Fixed a subtle bug in the draft keepalive mechanism where the sentinel value collided with legitimate empty content. The keepalive timer sets `lastRendered` to a special sentinel value to force a resend that extends the draft's 30-second TTL. Originally this used an empty string `""`, but this caused a bug during the initial "Thinking..." phase: when the session has no buffer, no thinking state, and no tool output yet, `renderDraftReply()` legitimately returns an empty string. The identical check would then match (empty sentinel === empty content) and skip the resend, allowing the draft to expire.

The fix changes the sentinel to `"\x00"` (a null character), which can never appear in legitimate rendered content. This guarantees the keepalive always triggers a resend, preventing draft expiration during long tool execution or waiting periods where no visible content has appeared yet. This edge case would have been difficult to notice except during slow responses where the agent stays in pure thinking mode for more than 30 seconds.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Committed draft streaming display fixes to repository</title>
  <subtitle>Packaged fixes for animation rollback and TTL expiration into commit c869dc9</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Committed 5 files with 243 insertions and 24 deletions addressing two Telegram draft streaming issues</fact>
    <fact>Issue 1: Visual rollback caused by Telegram's length-difference animation semantics when tools section inserted at message head</fact>
    <fact>Fix 1: New renderDraftReply() with body → thinking → tools ordering and markdown output instead of HTML</fact>
    <fact>Issue 2: Drafts disappearing after 30-second TTL during long tool execution or rate limiting</fact>
    <fact>Fix 2: 10-second keepalive timer with \x00 sentinel to bypass identical check, guarded by turnFinalized flag</fact>
    <fact>Added scripts/check-draft-render.mjs with 15 regression tests validating ordering, prefix stability, and token stripping</fact>
    <fact>SPEC-draft-streaming.md updated with real-world testing findings documenting two falsified design assumptions</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Committed comprehensive fixes for two Telegram draft streaming display issues discovered during real-world testing. The first issue involved visible text appearing to scroll backwards when new tool status lines appeared during agent execution. This was caused by Telegram's client-side animation behavior: the client uses "length-difference semantics" where it immediately shows the first N characters (N = previous text length) and fades in any additional characters. The original renderReply() placed tool status lines at the message head, so new tools inserted characters at position 0, causing the visible prefix to clip body text at the tail. The fix introduced renderDraftReply() with reordered sections (body → thinking → tools) for append-only frame behavior, plus markdown output instead of HTML to match Telegram's rich_message.markdown field.

The second issue involved drafts disappearing from the user's view after 30 seconds (Telegram's draft TTL), particularly during long tool execution with no output changes, content-unchanged early returns, or rate limiting periods. The fix added a 10-second keepalive timer that forcibly resends the draft with the same draft ID to extend its lifetime. The implementation uses a `\x00` null character as a sentinel value to bypass the identical-content check (preventing the bug where an empty sentinel would match legitimately empty content during the "Thinking..." phase), and guards with a turnFinalized flag to prevent ghost drafts after final message delivery.

The commit includes a comprehensive regression test suite (15 assertions covering ordering, prefix stability, markdown preservation, truncation, and token stripping), documentation updates capturing the falsified design assumptions from real-world testing, and inline code comments explaining the subtle edge cases. This represents a complete fix-test-document cycle for production-discovered UX issues.</narrative>

  </concepts>
